title: Decorative Panel Bed with Platform Frame (Queen)
summary: A queen platform bed under a moulding-framed 1x6 panel headboard — chair rail and base cap trim break the headboard into two tiers, mitered at every corner.
description:
This bed's headboard is the whole project: a field of ten 1x6 panel boards, trimmed at the edges in 1x2, sitting on a 2x6 base and flanked by 2x4 legs — and then wrapped in real moulding, chair rail on the lower tier and base cap on the upper, with every corner mitered at 45°. The moulding is intentionally left uncut in the source's own cut list — fit and cut it to your own assembled headboard rather than to a plan number, since moulding this visible has almost no tolerance for a gap.

The platform frame underneath is ordinary and sturdy: 2x4s screwed together like wall framing, joists spanning the width, and 1x8 siderails as the finished visible faces. The headboard attaches to the platform's legs after the fact, screwed but not glued, at a marked 14-1/2" leg height — deliberately no glue, in case the bed ever needs to come apart for a move.

Before committing to moulding cuts, test on scrap: 45° corners on real chair rail and base cap moulding are far less forgiving than the same angle on plain 1x stock, and a bad test cut costs a few inches of scrap rather than a piece of moulding you already bought for the visible corner of a headboard.

materials.name: Brad nails or wood trim screws, 1-1/4"
Chair rail moulding, 8 ft stick
materials.note: Bottom tier of the headboard trim.

materials.name: Base cap moulding, 8 ft stick
materials.note: Panel trim and top tier of the headboard.

materials.name: Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

cutList.note: Cut at 23-7/8" to account for the saw kerf if you need to conserve boards.
Cut at 20-7/8" if the panel boards are cut at 23-7/8".
Cut to fit your assembled panel — corners mitered at 45°.
Cut to fit — corners mitered at 45°.
Cut to fit — corners mitered at 45°.

steps.title: Build the headboard panel
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim to the panel boards, using screws from the back (1-1/4") to hide screw holes if you don't have a finish nailer. Use glue and square up the assembly as you go.
materials: Brad nails or wood trim screws, 1-1/4"
Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

steps.title: Attach the headboard base
steps.body: Attach the 2x6 base to the bottom of the panel. Predrill at an angle from the back if you only have a countersink bit, and use 2-1/2" screws and glue.
materials: Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

steps.title: Attach the legs and top
steps.body: Attach the headboard legs to the back of the panel the same way as the base. Then attach the 1x3 top panel — for extra confidence, a scrap 1/4" plywood strip across the top and legs from behind can back up a shallow toe-nailed angle screw.

steps.title: Add the moulding
steps.body: Fit and cut the chair rail and base cap moulding to your own assembled headboard and panel rather than to the cut list numbers — test 45° corner cuts on scrap moulding first. Glue and pin-nail in place if you have a finish nailer, or glue and clamp otherwise. Avoid letting glue dry on bare wood in areas that will be stained.
materials: Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

steps.title: Build the platform frame
steps.body: Screw the platform joists and sides together like wall framing, checking for square as you go. Use glue and 3" screws, countersunk.
materials: Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

steps.title: Add the siderails
steps.body: Attach the siderails and footrail, either screwed from the inside with 2" screws and glue, or finish-nailed from the outside.
materials: Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

steps.title: Attach the platform legs and assemble
steps.body: Build the platform legs and attach flush to the top of the platform frame. Mark the headboard legs at 14-1/2" and predrill from the back of the headboard into the platform frame. Attach with 3" screws and NO glue, so the bed can be disassembled later.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any exposed holes and imperfections with wood filler, let dry, sand with medium grit in the direction of the grain, and vacuum clean. Prime and paint, or stain, as desired. Add plywood or wood slats on top of the platform frame if using without a box spring.
materials: Wood glue, wood filler, and sandpaper

images.alt: Queen platform bed with a moulding-trimmed decorative panel headboard
